Who I am

A little about me

Hey! I'm Abdel Mahmoud. I'm a senior at Macaulay Honors College studying Information Systems and Informatics with a minor in Business, and honestly the path that got me here is a little all over the place but it makes sense when you hear it.

Growing up I really did want to be a hero. Like genuinely, not just as a kid thing. Batman, Superman, all of it. So when it came time to figure out what to do with my life I thought ok what's the most hero thing I can actually do, and I landed on medicine. I was planning on pursuing my goal with Macaulay Honors, pre-med, 4.0, the whole thing. And it just wasn't it. I could do the work but the passion wasn't there and I think I always kind of knew.

What I kept coming back to was data and AI. Every time I was messing with a dataset or building something that actually worked I felt it. So I transferred to CSI, switched into Information Systems, and it clicked immediately. Now I'm an AI Teaching Fellow at CodePath teaching 500+ students, I've built and deployed real projects, and I'm always working on something new.

My whole thing is impact. Not credit, not clout, just actually making things better for people. Whether it's something visible or something running behind the scenes that nobody ever thinks about. That to me is the real version of what I wanted to be when I was a kid reading comics.

"The satisfaction comes from helping people. That's what being a hero is all about."

Academic work

Research & Writing

Management Research · Dr. Jong Gyu Park · April 2025

AI Influence Among Gen Z Employees in Today's Hybrid and AI-Influenced Work Environments After the Pandemic

A research study conducted with Dr. Jong Gyu Park examining how COVID-19 reshaped the social behaviors and workplace dynamics of Gen Z. Through a survey of 30 CSI students across campus organizations, the paper tracks the measurable shift from extroversion toward ambiversion post-pandemic, and explores how Gen Z navigates AI tools at work — finding that while AI is seen as a powerful supplement, overreliance poses real risks to collaboration, traditional skills, and mental health.

More research and writing coming soon.

Projects

83 SHOW RECORDS · 2019–2025

Streaming Analytics Dashboard

Live ↗ GitHub ↗

Python · FastAPI · scikit-learn · Chart.js · SurveyMonkey API · Vercel

This is a end-to-end TV viewership analytics platform that uses and tracks 6 years of real Nielsen data across NBC and Peacock. It is with a Python FastAPI backend serving a Gradient Boosting model for show ratings predictions, linear regression forecasting with confidence intervals, an interactive What-If simulator for content strategy scenarios, and live SurveyMonkey survey integration. I also added the option of a groq api key usage for more catered responses.

Overdose Deaths Analysis — NYC

GitHub ↗

Python · Pandas · Excel · PowerBI

Pipeline ingesting 50,000+ CSV records across all five NYC boroughs with EDA visualizations. I made it to ask and answer the question of whether there was a correlation between overdose deaths and boroughs income. More of a test of relevant csv input data.
